text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Marcus T. Delgado
Medical Record Number: [MRN on file]
Date of Procedure: [see chart]
Date of Report: [see chart]
Attending Hematopathologist: [on file]
Ordering Clinician: [Hematology/Oncology, Inpatient Service]
Clinical Indication: Response assessment following induction chemotherapy for AML. Patient is a 48-year-old male with de novo AML (FAB M1, AML without maturation), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (AR 0.87), DNMT3A R882H co-mutation, ELN 2022 adverse-risk classification. Received 7+3 induction (cytarabine 100 mg/m2/day days 1-7 continuous infusion, daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV bolus days 1-3) with midostaurin 50 mg PO twice daily days 8-21. This biopsy performed at approximately day 28-30 post-induction initiation to assess remission status.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: Right posterior iliac crest — core biopsy
Specimen B: Left posterior iliac crest — core biopsy
Specimen C: Bone marrow aspirate smears and clot section — bilateral posterior iliac crest

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TYPE OF SPECIMEN
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Bilateral posterior iliac crest bone marrow core biopsies and aspirate smears. Procedure performed by the hematology/oncology fellow under attending supervision at the bedside under local anesthesia (lidocaine 1% injection). Jamshidi needle utilized for core biopsies. Aspirate obtained via standard technique; 6 mL aspirated for smears, clot section, flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS PATHOLOGY DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A (Right core): Received in formalin, a single cylindrical core of bone and marrow tissue measuring 1.5 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The core is tan-pink, soft to firm, and intact. Submitted entirely in one cassette for decalcification and paraffin embedding.

Specimen B (Left core): Received in formalin, a single cylindrical core of bone and marrow tissue measuring 1.4 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The core is tan-pink with focal areas of red discoloration consistent with blood. Core is intact without fragmentation. Submitted entirely in one cassette for decalcification and paraffin embedding.

Specimen C (Aspirate): Received fresh, multiple air-dried and methanol-fixed aspirate smears prepared from bilateral aspirate. Clot section prepared and submitted in formalin. Residual material submitted for flow cytometry immunophenotyping (sent fresh to flow laboratory), cytogenetics (sent in heparinized media), and molecular studies (sent in EDTA for PCR and MRD analysis). Smears are adequate with visible spicules on the majority of slides.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CORE BIOPSIES (Specimens A and B — Hematoxylin and Eosin, PAS, Reticulin, and Immunohistochemical stains performed):

Both core biopsies are adequate and evaluable, with cortical bone present at the specimen ends confirming sampling of posterior iliac crest marrow. The cores are representative of post-chemotherapy marrow.

Cellularity: The marrow is markedly hypocellular, with estimated cellularity of 5-10% (reference range for age: 40-60%). The hypocellularity is diffuse and uniform throughout both cores, consistent with expected post-chemotherapy aplasia following myeloablative induction with 7+3 regimen. Fat spaces are markedly expanded and occupy the majority of the marrow space.

Blasts: No morphologically identifiable blasts are seen on the core biopsy sections. Scattered cells present are consistent with early recovering hematopoietic precursors and stromal elements. No clusters of immature cells or sheet-like proliferations of blasts are identified. No Auer rods are identified.

Erythropoiesis: Markedly reduced but recovering. Early erythroid precursors including proerythroblasts and basophilic normoblasts are present in small clusters, predominantly in a perivascular distribution, consistent with early erythropoietic reconstitution. No dysplastic erythroid forms (nuclear budding, karyorrhexis, multinucleation) are identified.

Myelopoiesis: Markedly reduced but beginning to recover. Early myeloid precursors (promyelocytes, myelocytes) are present in small numbers. No maturation arrest. No hypogranulation or pseudo-Pelger-Huet anomaly is identified to suggest dysplasia.

Megakaryocytes: Present in reduced but non-absent numbers. The megakaryocytes identified are morphologically unremarkable with appropriate multilobated nuclei. No micromegakaryocytes, hypolobulated megakaryocytes, or clustering are identified. No dysplastic megakaryocyte forms.

Stroma and vascularity: Bone marrow stroma is intact. Small vessels with normal morphology are present. No hemophagocytic activity. No granulomas. No necrosis. Bony trabeculae are morphologically normal.

Reticulin stain: Grade 0-1 reticulin fibrosis (European Consensus grading scale). Focal fine reticulin fibers present without intersections. No collagen fibrosis (trichrome stain negative for collagen deposition). This is essentially within normal limits and represents a decrease from the original biopsy where no significant fibrosis was noted; findings are consistent with post-chemotherapy aplastic marrow.

ASPIRATE SMEARS AND CLOT SECTION (Specimen C — Wright-Giemsa stain):

The aspirate smears are adequate, with visible spicules and good cellular morphology. The differential count is performed on a 500-cell differential of nucleated marrow elements.

Aspirante Differential (500-cell count):
- Myeloblasts: 1.2% (less than 5%; within normal limits)
- Promyelocytes: 3.8%
- Myelocytes/metamyelocytes: 12.4%
- Band forms/segmented neutrophils: 18.6%
- Eosinophils/eosinophil precursors: 2.2%
- Basophils: 0.4%
- Erythroid precursors (all stages): 38.8%
- Lymphocytes: 14.6%
- Monocytes: 4.2%
- Plasma cells: 1.8%
- Megakaryocytes: present (not included in 500-cell differential)

Blast count: 1.2% by morphology (less than 5%, consistent with complete morphologic remission per ELN 2022 and standard AML response criteria). No Auer rods identified. No aberrant blast morphology.

The blast population, where present, is small and heterogeneous in appearance, consistent with normal residual hematopoietic progenitors rather than leukemic blasts. No monotonous blast proliferation.

Erythroid precursors show normal maturation sequence without dysplasia. Myeloid precursors show appropriate granulation and maturation. No ring sideroblasts (iron stain performed: Prussian blue negative for ring sideroblasts; storage iron present as scattered macrophage hemosiderin, consistent with transfusion-associated iron stores).

Clot section: Consistent with core biopsy findings. Hypocellular with recovering trilineage hematopoiesis. No blast aggregates.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on paraffin sections of core biopsies):

- CD34 (clone QBEnd/10): Highlights rare scattered progenitor cells, estimated less than 1% of marrow cellularity. No CD34+ blast clusters or sheets identified. Normal vascular endothelial staining present as internal positive control. Pattern is consistent with normal residual hematopoietic progenitors; this is markedly decreased compared to the original diagnostic biopsy where CD34+ blasts comprised the dominant marrow population.

- CD117 (c-Kit; clone 104D2): Rare positive cells consistent with residual mast cells and early myeloid precursors. No aberrant CD117 overexpression or blast aggregates. No aberrant co-expression of CD34 and CD117 by dual staining.

- Myeloperoxidase (MPO; polyclonal): Positive in maturing myeloid elements, appropriate distribution. No MPO-positive blast clusters.

- CD3 (clone SP7): Scattered T-lymphocytes in perivascular and interstitial distribution. No T-cell aggregates or atypical lymphoid infiltrates.

- CD20 (clone L26): Rare B-lymphocytes, no B-cell aggregates or follicles.

- TdT (clone SEN28): NEGATIVE. No TdT-positive blasts identified. (Negative result supports absence of lymphoblastic infiltrate.)

- CD64: Negative on blast population.

- Glycophorin C (clone Ret40f): Confirms presence of erythroid island recovery in perivascular areas.

FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (Specimen C, aspirate — 10-color multiparameter panel; reported separately by flow cytometry laboratory, results incorporated here for integrated diagnosis):

Result: MRD NEGATIVE. No leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) detected. Residual blasts (CD34+/CD117+ cells) comprise less than 0.01% of total nucleated cells analyzed (below the sensitivity threshold of 0.01% = 10^-4). Prior leukemic phenotype (CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/HLA-DR+/MPO+/CD14-/CD64-) is not detectable. Recovering normal hematopoiesis with appropriate immunophenotypic maturation is present in all three lineages.

This MRD result was performed simultaneously with morphology and is consistent with complete morphologic remission.

CYTOGENETICS (conventional G-band karyotype from bone marrow aspirate; analysis performed by cytogenetics laboratory):

Result: 46,XY [20/20 metaphases analyzed]
Interpretation: Normal male karyotype. No cytogenetic abnormalities detected. No clonal abnormalities identified. This represents persistence of the normal karyotype seen at diagnosis (no new cytogenetic abnormalities have emerged). The original karyotype at diagnosis was also 46,XY (normal diploid), confirming the cytogenetically normal AML subtype.

MOLECULAR STUDIES — PCR (performed on bone marrow aspirate DNA):

FLT3-ITD PCR (fragment analysis, capillary electrophoresis):
Result: NEGATIVE for FLT3-ITD.
Methodology: PCR amplification across the FLT3 exon 14-15 ITD region with fluorescently labeled primers followed by capillary electrophoresis. Sensitivity of assay: approximately 1-2% (allelic ratio threshold). No aberrant fragment(s) detected in the expected size range for ITD insertion. Wild-type FLT3 allele present. This represents clearance of the FLT3-ITD that was detected at diagnosis (baseline allelic ratio 0.87, VAF 47%).

Note: DNMT3A R882H by digital droplet PCR (ddPCR): PENDING at time of this report. Expected sensitivity ~0.1% VAF. Results to be reported as an addendum when available.

Note: Comprehensive MRD by NGS (error-corrected sequencing for FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A R882H, PTPN11 E76K): PENDING. Results to be reported as addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE, BILATERAL POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST (RESPONSE ASSESSMENT, POST-INDUCTION DAY 28-30):**

1. ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA — COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC REMISSION (CR1).
   - Blast count by morphology: 1.2% (less than 5%; meets morphologic CR criteria per ELN 2022 and IWG response criteria).
   - No morphologic evidence of residual AML.
   - Post-chemotherapy aplasia with early trilineage hematopoietic recovery.

2. MINIMAL RESIDUAL DISEASE (MRD) BY MULTIPARAMETER FLOW CYTOMETRY: NEGATIVE.
   - No leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected.
   - Sensitivity: 10^-4 (0.01%).

3. CYTOGENETICS: 46,XY — normal male karyotype, no cytogenetic abnormalities.

4. FLT3-ITD BY PCR: NEGATIVE (clearance of previously detected FLT3-ITD with allelic ratio 0.87).

5. DNMT3A R882H BY ddPCR: PENDING — ADDENDUM TO FOLLOW.

6. RETICULIN FIBROSIS: Grade 0-1 (minimal), within normal limits.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGN-OUT PATHOLOGIST
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[Attending Hematopathologist signature and credentials on file]
Case reviewed and signed out by board-certified hematopathologist.
Electronic signature applied.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDENDUM (to be completed upon receipt of pending molecular results)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Addendum pending: DNMT3A R882H ddPCR and comprehensive MRD-NGS results. Results will be incorporated into the final integrated report and communicated to the treating hematology/oncology team.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL CORRELATION NOTE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The morphologic, immunophenotypic, cytogenetic, and available molecular findings are consistent with complete remission following 7+3 induction chemotherapy with midostaurin. The marrow hypocellularity is expected at this post-induction timepoint and represents the nadir of chemotherapy-induced aplasia with early recovery. Trilineage hematopoiesis is recovering. Clearance of FLT3-ITD by PCR is a favorable prognostic indicator, although the sensitivity of fragment-analysis PCR (approximately 1-2%) is substantially lower than NGS-based or ddPCR-based methods; pending MRD-NGS results should be considered for comprehensive MRD assessment. Given the ELN 2022 adverse-risk classification (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H co-mutation), clinical management decisions including consolidative therapy should be made in the context of the complete molecular MRD assessment when available.

[End of Report]
